clusters.title: Woman dead, crime scene declared at Gold Coast fire, as man arrested over separate blaze north of Brisbane
clusters.neutral_headline: Queensland unit fire claims woman's life
bullet_summary: A person has been found dead and a crime scene has been declared after a unit fire on the Gold Coast

items.title: Woman dead, crime scene declared at Gold Coast fire, as man arrested over separate blaze north of Brisbane
items.standfirst: An elderly woman has died after they were pulled from a unit fire on the Gold Coast, prompting a police investigation, while a man has been arrested following a blaze that engulfed three Moreton Bay homes.
items.content: An elderly woman has died after they were pulled from a unit fire on the Gold Coast, prompting a police investigation, while a man has been arrested following a blaze that engulfed three Moreton Bay homes.

items.title: Queensland unit fire claims woman's life
items.standfirst: A person has been found dead and a crime scene has been declared after a unit fire on the Gold Coast.
items.content: An elderly woman has been found dead and a crime scene has been declared after a unit fire on the Gold Coast.The fire broke out at a complex on Surf Parade in Broadbeach at about 11am today.When emergency services arrived at the scene, the unit was fully engulfed and the fire was extinguised a short time later.READ MORE: Villager trapped in flooded cave pulled out alive Authorities found the body during a search of the property. CPR was performed on the resident but they were declared deceased a short time later.The person has not been publicly identified. No other injuries have been reported following the fire.It is the second major blaze authorities have been called to in Queensland today. A fire also broke out at a unit on Weaber Street in Clontarf at about 2pm.Residents were asked to stay inside and keep their windows closed due to the smoke.

clusters.title: Man left for dead after asking for music to be turned down
clusters.neutral_headline: Man left for dead after asking for music to be turned down

items.title: Man left for dead after asking for music to be turned down
items.standfirst: A Sydney man has been bashed and left for dead in a park near his home after telling a group of partiers to turn the music down.
items.content: A Sydney man has been bashed and left for dead in a park near his home after telling a group of partiers to turn the music down.

items.title: Man beaten and left for dead after asking partiers to turn down music
items.standfirst: A Sydney man has been beaten up and left for dead in a park near his home - after telling a group of partiers to turn the music down.
items.content: A Sydney man has been beaten up and left for dead in a park near his home after telling a group of partiers to turn the music down.The 47-year-old is stable in hospital after being found by neighbours but the incident has rattled the community who fear the worse is yet to come.Late last night a 47-year-old who lives in a unit next to James Meehan reserve in Dee Why on the Northern Beaches called out to a group partying in the park, asking them to turn the music down.READ MORE: Professor Richard Scolyer's wife shares sad update on his brain cancer battleOne witness said they "had a boom box" which was "really, really loud at one stage."Another said the man had been shouting from his balcony.He went down to ask again but police say he was surrounded and bashed, ending up in hospital with facial injuries."He believes he was lying there for an hour but we're to investigate that properly," Inspector Vanessa Robinson from NSW Police said.READ MORE: Vivid Sydney show scrapped for good after drone chaosCouncillor Vincent De Luca hopes the perpetrators are found quickly."NSW Police need to get onto this pretty quick, our community expect arrests and expect justice," he said."Locals are petrified to even take their dogs for a walk."We have an epidemic of gang crime and that must be addressed." READ MORE: Free tickets to soldiers for Trump's UFC event, but only if they are thin enough Incidents where a group target an individual is not uncommon on the Northern Beaches.Some locals spoken to by 9News said they were scared, but Inspector Robinson maintained it was safe to go to parks.
